free_text: Narrow alpha + 28Si elastic-scattering states at high
excitation in 32S.
K-20 isochronous cyclotron in the
particle energy range 12.0-19.6 MeV.
in the range of 6.5-12.8 MeV beam
energy.
K-35 cyclotron in the energy range
Target was 10 micron thick plate of natural silicon.
Isotope abundances are 28Si 92.2, 29Si 4.7 and 30Si
3.1% respectively.
105 degree analyzing magnet at K-20 cyclotron, 90
degree magnet at accelerator, 50 degree analyzing
magnet at K-35 cyclotron were used for beam energies
analyzing.
The excitation functions were measured with a set of
seven detectors in three settings, giving 21 angular-
distribution points in the angle range 107-173 degrees.
No information about the detector type used is given.
Backscattering technique on a thick target.
Partial spectra were corrected for energy loss in
the target and matched by normalizing the intensity.
Intrinsic detector resolution was about 20 keV.
SD: angles in Subent 002 corrected for
energies 12.68, 14.07

Individual measured spectra for different angles
were energy shifted and merged into two-dimensional
Ecm x ANGcm matrices. Excitation function was analyzed
by fitting individual peaks with individual widths
together with background in the 173 degree spectrum. In
this manner resonance energies and widths were
extracted.
Uncertainty in resonance width.
